audit_items.proof_trace: The function has no internal NULL guards for `value` or `value->bv_val`, but both callers guarantee non-NULL inputs: `ldif.c:178` passes `&value` (stack struct) with `bv_val` set to a non-NULL parsed buffer (empty-string case returns early at L167-174); `tls2.c:1070` passes `&bv` (stack struct) with `bv_val` set from a non-NULL `LDAP_STRDUP` result (NULL allocation crashes at `strchr(NULL,':')` before the decode call). The null-terminator write at L105 is within the original input buffer since base64 decoding always yields fewer bytes than the input.
